QF.id: XVI Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_56

question.text: Index Futures and Index Options are settled by:

answer.text: Cash settlement — delivery period margins do not apply to index derivatives
Compulsory physical delivery of index constituent commodities
Both options (buyer and seller must agree)
A combination of cash and physical delivery

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Index derivatives are always cash-settled because you cannot physically deliver an 'index.' Since there's no delivery, delivery period margins don't apply. **The Trap:** Physical delivery of an index is impossible. 'Both options' doesn't apply to indices. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers equity index settlement. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Cash settlement makes index derivatives simpler to manage — no warehouse logistics, quality issues, or delivery default risk. This is why institutional investors prefer index instruments.
